library.label: Library Access
library.notes: Annual survey of all US public library administrative entities. County totals aggregated from library-level data using Census tract FIPS mapping.

mobility.label: Economic Mobility
mobility.unit: mean household income rank at age 35 (percentile)
mobility.notes: Tracks income outcomes of children born 1978–1983, measured at age ~35 (through 2015). Reflects historical structural conditions, not current mobility. Significant temporal mismatch when compared to 2022 datasets. Based on linked Census–IRS administrative records.

air.label: Air Quality
air.notes: Annual county AQI summary from EPA monitoring stations. Only 973 counties with 50+ monitoring days included. Counties without monitors are absent, not zero. Monitor placement is non-random.

broadband.label: Broadband Access
broadband.notes: ACS 5-year estimates represent a 2018–2022 average, not a single point in time. Broadband subscription rate = B28002_004E / B28002_001E.

eitc.label: EITC Uptake
eitc.notes: ZIP-code level tax filing data aggregated to county via Census ZCTA-county area-weighted crosswalk. Area weighting assumes uniform population density within ZCTAs.

poverty.label: Poverty Rate
poverty.source: Census SAIPE (Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates)
poverty.notes: Model-based estimates combining ACS survey data, IRS tax returns, and SNAP administrative records. Designed specifically for county-level estimates.

median_income.label: Median Income
median_income.notes: Model-based county estimates from Census SAIPE program. Same source as poverty rate.

bea_income.label: Per Capita Income
bea_income.notes: ACS 5-year estimate (2018–2022 average). Labeled 'BEA Income' in the UI but sourced from Census ACS, not BEA CAINC tables, because BEA API requires registration.

food_access.label: Food Access (SNAP Proxy)
food_access.notes: SNAP receipt rate used as food access proxy. The original USDA Food Access Research Atlas (which measures physical proximity to grocery stores) was unavailable (URLs returned 404). SNAP receipt measures food insecurity, not food desert geography.

obesity.label: Obesity Rate
obesity.notes: Crude prevalence from CDC PLACES, derived from BRFSS survey data with small-area estimation. 2,957 counties covered. Year is the release year; underlying BRFSS data may span 2020–2022.

diabetes.label: Diabetes Rate
diabetes.notes: Crude prevalence from CDC PLACES. Same methodology and coverage as obesity. Small-area estimates from BRFSS telephone survey.

mental_health.label: Mental Health
mental_health.unit: % of adults with frequent mental distress
mental_health.notes: Crude prevalence from CDC PLACES. Measures ≥14 poor mental health days in past 30 days. Self-reported via telephone survey.

hypertension.label: Hypertension Rate
hypertension.unit: % of adults with high blood pressure
hypertension.notes: Crude prevalence from CDC PLACES. Same methodology as other health measures.

unemployment.label: Unemployment Rate
unemployment.notes: ACS 5-year estimate (2018–2022 average). BLS Local Area Unemployment Statistics (which provide monthly point-in-time data) were unavailable due to automated access restrictions.

rural_urban.label: Rural/Urban Classification
rural_urban.notes: 2023 vintage (latest available). USDA updates these codes roughly every 10 years following each Census. Based on 2020 Census population and OMB metro area definitions.

housing_burden.label: Housing Burden
housing_burden.unit: % of renters paying 30%+ of income on rent
housing_burden.notes: ACS 5-year estimate. Covers renters only, not homeowners. Housing cost burden threshold (30%) is the HUD standard definition.

voter_turnout.label: Voter Turnout
voter_turnout.notes: 2020 presidential election county returns. Raw vote count, not turnout rate (eligible voter denominator not available at county level in this source). 3,152 counties covered.
